builder: Claude Code S4_BUILD executor, session to be selected before S4
builder_boundary: External builder may only own S4_BUILD after S3 implementation plan; it may not edit spec, verification plan, verifier, release, or push authority files.

verify_arch.reason: Spec declares multiple surfaces: skills, hooks/API, local orch handoff, tests, file artifacts, message disclosure.

acceptance.then: use superpower enters visible FSM before ad hoc execution
two-pass design loop cannot skip SOTA before final spec
verification-plan/test-design is required before implementation plan
Codex guidance avoids nonexistent Skill callable while Claude keeps Skill tool
active writing-arch references are absent or historical only
Claude builder can own implementation only after approved plan
prompt and gate behavior has deterministic regression coverage
substantial specs produce review artifacts or explicit N/A reason
all user-raised issues have durable multidimensional elicitation coverage
pause and transition responses include compact progress line

cases.given: SFSM-01 is evaluated from the approved Capability Registry, not from the implementation plan
cases.when: the verifier exercises the happy condition for using-superpowers skill
cases.then: the nominal path delivers the declared outcome: use superpower enters visible FSM before ad hoc execution

cases.given: SFSM-01 is evaluated from the approved Capability Registry, not from the implementation plan
cases.when: the verifier exercises the boundary condition for using-superpowers skill
cases.then: the smallest valid prompt and the full remaining gate list still deliver the declared outcome: use superpower enters visible FSM before ad hoc execution

cases.given: SFSM-01 is evaluated from the approved Capability Registry, not from the implementation plan
cases.when: the verifier exercises the negative condition for using-superpowers skill
cases.then: premature or incomplete action is blocked instead of silently advancing: use superpower enters visible FSM before ad hoc execution

cases.given: SFSM-01 is evaluated from the approved Capability Registry, not from the implementation plan
cases.when: the verifier exercises the integration condition for using-superpowers skill
cases.then: the declared surface and adjacent hook/artifact cooperate to deliver the outcome: use superpower enters visible FSM before ad hoc execution

cases.given: SFSM-01 is evaluated from the approved Capability Registry, not from the implementation plan
cases.when: the verifier exercises the corner condition for using-superpowers skill
cases.then: combined runtime, delegation, and gate-state variations preserve the outcome: use superpower enters visible FSM before ad hoc execution

cases.given: SFSM-01 is evaluated from the approved Capability Registry, not from the implementation plan
cases.when: the verifier exercises the error-guessing condition for using-superpowers skill
cases.then: a known failure pattern is targeted and caught by the guard: use superpower enters visible FSM before ad hoc execution

cases.given: SFSM-01 is evaluated from the approved Capability Registry, not from the implementation plan
cases.when: an unscripted charter probes trigger phrasing beyond the scripted cases: mid-conversation trigger after unrelated topics, embedded trigger inside a longer sentence, unusual capitalization, and repeated triggers in the same session
cases.then: for at least 3 unscripted trigger variants, the first substantive reply names S0_DISCUSS as current state and lists all stages S0_DISCUSS through S6_RELEASE with their skill mapping every time; any variant producing a partial stage list or a skipped recap is a defect the scripted happy/boundary cases would not catch

cases.given: SFSM-01 is evaluated from the approved Capability Registry, not from the implementation plan
cases.when: the trigger phrase is sent and every tool call made before the comprehension-gate recap is delivered is inspected
cases.then: the forbidden state is a spec, plan, or code file written before S0_DISCUSS completes; the oracle is zero Write/Edit tool calls creating spec/plan/code artifacts occurring before the recap is emitted in the transcript; any such write appearing before the recap means the forbidden state occurred

cases.given: SFSM-02 is evaluated from the approved Capability Registry, not from the implementation plan
cases.when: an unscripted charter tries adversarial phrasings that request an immediate final spec ("just finalize the spec now", supplying a spec that already reads as final, asking to skip research) without ever naming S1_SOTA
cases.then: across at least 3 adversarial phrasings, the gate transcript shows S1_SOTA source-research activity recorded before any S1_SPEC_FINAL artifact is produced in every run; a run that produces S1_SPEC_FINAL with no preceding S1_SOTA evidence is a failure the scripted negative case would not surface

cases.given: SFSM-02 is evaluated from the approved Capability Registry, not from the implementation plan
cases.when: every session reaching S1_SPEC_FINAL is scanned for a preceding S1_SOTA entry
cases.then: the forbidden state is S1_SOTA silently marked complete or skipped without recorded research evidence; the oracle is that zero S1_SPEC_FINAL entries in the run corpus have no preceding S1_SOTA entry with cited sources; any S1_SPEC_FINAL lacking that predecessor means the forbidden state occurred

cases.given: SFSM-03 is evaluated from the approved Capability Registry, not from the implementation plan
cases.when: an unscripted charter requests "write the implementation plan" directly after spec approval with no mention of verification, and separately submits an implementation-plan document for review while no test-design.json exists yet
cases.then: in each probe the gate redirects to S2_VERIFICATION_PLAN before accepting S3_IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN content; no probe reaches an accepted implementation plan while test-design.json is missing or absent from the run

cases.given: SFSM-03 is evaluated from the approved Capability Registry, not from the implementation plan
cases.when: run history is checked for the relative ordering of the S3_IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN artifact against the S2 verification artifact's git state (mirrors the S2-artifacts-dirty check in tests/test-superpower-fsm-runtime.ps1 step 4)
cases.then: the forbidden state is an accepted S3_IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN artifact whose timestamp precedes or coincides with a missing or dirty .superpowers/verify/test-design.json; the oracle is that this ordering never appears in the logged transitions for a passing run

cases.given: SFSM-04 is evaluated from the approved Capability Registry, not from the implementation plan
cases.when: an unscripted charter feeds validate_skill_invocation_guidance additional excerpt shapes beyond the scripted fixture: a mid-file quote, a code-fenced excerpt, and a reference-link context, for both the codex and claude harness arguments
cases.then: across at least 3 additional excerpt variants, the codex-targeted validation never returns a defect naming a nonexistent Skill callable, and the claude-targeted validation still requires the Skill tool phrase to be present; any variant flipping either guarantee is a failure the scripted cases would not surface

cases.given: SFSM-04 is evaluated from the approved Capability Registry, not from the implementation plan
cases.when: validate_skill_invocation_guidance("codex", text) is run over the full using-superpowers/SKILL.md and every file under skills/using-superpowers/references/
cases.then: the forbidden state is Codex guidance instructing invocation of a nonexistent "Skill" tool callable; the oracle is that this defect string is never returned for any accepted guidance file in the corpus

cases.given: SFSM-05 is evaluated from the approved Capability Registry, not from the implementation plan
cases.when: an unscripted charter greps the whole repository for "writing-arch", not just the four paths (skills, hooks, lib, docs, FORK-MAINTENANCE.md) scanned by tests/test-superpower-fsm-runtime.ps1 step 3, including README.md, .github/, and evals/
cases.then: a full-repo grep for "writing-arch" outside the four scripted paths returns zero hits, or every hit found is qualified by historical/superseded/obsolete on the same line; any unscoped active hit is a defect the scripted scan would miss

cases.given: SFSM-05 is evaluated from the approved Capability Registry, not from the implementation plan
cases.when: tests/test-superpower-fsm-runtime.ps1 step 3's active-reference collection ($active) is inspected after a clean run
cases.then: the forbidden state is an active (non-historical-qualified) "writing-arch" reference surviving anywhere in the repo; the oracle is that $active.Count is 0 for a passing regression run and never observed above 0

cases.given: SFSM-06 is evaluated from the approved Capability Registry, not from the implementation plan
cases.when: an unscripted charter mutates the scripted valid builder_job payload beyond the single happy fixture: user_specified flipped to false after a named session was requested, session_name changed after plan approval, and handoff_channel omitted for a claude_web session
cases.then: across at least 3 mutated payloads, validate_builder_job returns a non-empty defect list for every mutation that violates confirming the executor before S4_BUILD; a mutated payload silently returning an empty list is a failure the scripted cases would not surface

cases.given: SFSM-06 is evaluated from the approved Capability Registry, not from the implementation plan
cases.when: every accepted builder_job in the job/outbox log is cross-referenced against the session name confirmed in the prior gate transcript
cases.then: the forbidden state is the S4_BUILD executor substituted for a different session or engine than the one confirmed, without a new explicit confirmation step; the oracle is that no accepted job has builder_session.session_name diverge from the confirmed name while user_specified is true, and this divergence never appears in the accepted-job log

cases.given: SFSM-07 is evaluated from the approved Capability Registry, not from the implementation plan
cases.when: tests/test-superpower-fsm-runtime.ps1 is run twice in a row on an unchanged working tree, and its output is diffed for hidden order-dependence from leftover __pycache__ or temp files
cases.then: both runs produce identical PASS/FAIL outcomes for every step and an identical case count printed at step 2 ("test-design.json lint OK (N cases)"); any run-to-run divergence in outcome or reported count is a defect the single scripted run would not surface

cases.given: SFSM-07 is evaluated from the approved Capability Registry, not from the implementation plan
cases.when: the $LASTEXITCODE value is inspected immediately after every pytest invocation inside tests/test-superpower-fsm-runtime.ps1
cases.then: the forbidden state is the script printing a step "PASSED" line while the preceding pytest invocation's $LASTEXITCODE was non-zero; the oracle is that this combination never appears in the script's transcript, since a non-zero exit throws before the PASSED line prints

cases.given: SFSM-08 is evaluated from the approved Capability Registry, not from the implementation plan
cases.when: an unscripted charter probes validate_visual_artifact_policy with borderline-substantial specs: an empty visual_artifacts list with no n/a_reason, a visual_artifacts entry pointing at a missing file path, and a visual_artifacts list containing only an .html file when spec_type FSM expects PNG/SVG
cases.then: for at least 3 boundary specs beyond the scripted fixture, validate_visual_artifact_policy returns a non-empty defect list whenever both a real artifact and an explicit N/A reason are absent; any boundary spec silently passing with neither is a failure

cases.given: SFSM-08 is evaluated from the approved Capability Registry, not from the implementation plan
cases.when: every spec/artifact combination accepted by validate_visual_artifact_policy is inspected for the pairing of an empty artifacts list with no n/a_reason field
cases.then: the forbidden state is a substantial spec accepted with neither a visual artifact nor an explicit N/A reason recorded; the oracle is that validate_visual_artifact_policy never returns an empty defect list (pass) for that exact combination

cases.given: SFSM-09 is evaluated from the approved Capability Registry, not from the implementation plan
cases.when: an unscripted charter injects issues raised late in the conversation (after S1_SPEC_FINAL) or phrased only informally inside a pause/approval message, never added to a tracked issue list, and checks whether coverage tracking still captures them
cases.then: for at least 3 late-raised or informally phrased issues, the issue-coverage validator either records them as tracked or explicitly flags them as missing coverage; an injected issue that disappears from the coverage report with no recorded defect is a failure the scripted cases would not surface

cases.given: SFSM-09 is evaluated from the approved Capability Registry, not from the implementation plan
cases.when: every issue mention in the conversation transcript is cross-referenced against the tracked-entry list in the issue-coverage artifact
cases.then: the forbidden state is a user-raised issue present in the transcript but absent from the issue-coverage artifact with no recorded defect explaining the gap; the oracle is that zero such silently dropped issues appear in the artifact for a passing run

cases.given: SFSM-10 is evaluated from the approved Capability Registry, not from the implementation plan
cases.when: an unscripted charter exercises compact_progress across gate/skill/owner combinations beyond the single scripted S0_DISCUSS example (S1_SOTA, S4_BUILD with an @owner suffix, S5_VERIFY_ARCH, S6_RELEASE) and separately simulates a routine same-gate tool-call message that should not re-trigger the line
cases.then: for at least 3 additional gate/skill/owner combinations, compact_progress produces a line starting "Superpower: now=<gate>(<skill>" with already-passed gates omitted and non-current owners stated explicitly; a simulated routine same-gate tool-call message does not produce a duplicate progress line

cases.given: SFSM-10 is evaluated from the approved Capability Registry, not from the implementation plan
cases.when: every pause/transition response's progress line is checked against the required "Superpower: now=<gate>(<skill>[ @owner]); next=<gate>(<skill>) > ..." pattern with a real gate name substituted for the placeholder
cases.then: the forbidden state is the progress line replaced by generic boilerplate that does not name the actual current gate/skill/owner; the oracle is that no pause/transition response in the corpus is found with the literal placeholder text or a fixed generic string in place of a real gate name
